title: Nasdaq 100 Rallies, Oil Sinks To $75 On Iran Peace Deal: Stock Market Today
author: Piero Cingari
description: U.S. stocks rebounded Thursday with the Nasdaq 100 leading gains on a semiconductor rally fueled by an Intel-Apple chip manufacturing partnership. Oil prices tumbled 2.2% to $75/barrel following a U.S.-Iran peace deal and Strait of Hormuz reopening. The S&P 500 rose 1.2% while the Russell 2000 fell 0.7% amid mixed market performance.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surged 9.8% following announcement of Apple partnership to design and build chips in America, extending its strong 400%+ run since 2025 government investment
Benefited from semiconductor sector rally and positive sentiment from Intel partnership announcement, though modest 0.49% gain reflects broader tech strength
Rose 3.9% in sympathy with Intel's surge and broader semiconductor sector strength
Gained 4.24% as semiconductor peers rallied on Intel-Apple deal and AI-silicon momentum
Climbed 12.1% riding the AI-silicon rally and semiconductor sector strength
Added 11.5% benefiting from AI-silicon rally and semiconductor sector momentum
Rose 11.0% on strength in memory pricing within the semiconductor sector
Plunged 17.4% after cutting fiscal-year revenue-growth guidance due to cautious enterprise spending and slowdown in U.S. federal business
Fell 10.4% on Accenture's negative guidance read-across affecting the entire digital-consulting cohort
Dropped 9.9% due to Accenture's cautious outlook impacting the consulting sector
Lost 9.8% as consulting sector repriced following Accenture's revenue guidance cut
Slid 8.7% on Accenture's negative read-across to the IT services and consulting industry

title: What to Know About This Fund's $39 Million EPAM Exit After a 43% Stock Decline
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Owls Nest Partners IA liquidated its entire 230,246-share position in EPAM Systems for approximately $39.37 million as the stock declined 43% over the past year. Despite EPAM showing operational improvements in Q1 2026 with 7.6% revenue growth and raised full-year guidance, the fund chose to exit entirely rather than wait for recovery.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock has declined 43% over the past year, significantly underperforming the S&P 500. A major fund completely exited its position, signaling loss of confidence. However, underlying business metrics show improvement with strong Q1 results, raised guidance, and management optimism about AI-driven growth, creating mixed signals for investors.

title: This Investor Is Betting $16 Million on a Software Stock Down 17% This Past Year. Here's What to Know
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Blue Door Asset Management added 88,100 shares of NICE (valued at $10.64 million) in Q4 2025, bringing its total position to 142,900 shares worth $16.15 million. Despite NICE stock declining 16.5% over the past year, the company showed strong fundamentals with 8% revenue growth to $2.95 billion, 13% cloud segment expansion, and 43% EPS growth in 2025, with AI products driving accelerating adoption.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite recent stock underperformance (-16.5% YoY), NICE demonstrates strong operational fundamentals with 8% revenue growth, 13% cloud segment expansion, 43% EPS growth, and accelerating AI product adoption (66% ARR surge). Institutional investor confidence is evidenced by Blue Door Asset Management's $10.64 million additional investment, suggesting the stock may be undervalued relative to its business performance.
FLEX is mentioned as Blue Door Asset Management's top holding ($23.49 million, 14.7% of AUM) but no specific company information or performance data is provided in the article.
EPAM is listed as the second-largest holding in Blue Door's portfolio ($18.38 million, 11.5% of AUM) but no specific company information or performance data is provided in the article.

title: Waystar Stock Is Down 32% This Past Year, but One Fund Bought Up $11.5 Million Last Quarter
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Despite Waystar's stock declining 31.5% over the past year, Blue Door Asset Management purchased 322,600 shares worth $11.49 million in the latest quarter. The healthcare software company reported strong fundamentals with $1.10 billion in revenue (up 17% YoY) and adjusted EBITDA of $462 million (up 21% YoY), suggesting institutional confidence despite market weakness in software stocks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite significant stock price decline, the company demonstrates strong operational performance with 17% revenue growth and 21% EBITDA growth. Institutional fund buying signals confidence in fundamentals, and forward guidance projects continued growth to $1.28 billion in revenue.
Mentioned as Blue Door's largest holding at 14.7% of AUM; no specific news or performance commentary provided.
Listed as second-largest holding at 11.5% of AUM; no specific news or performance commentary provided.
Mentioned as third-largest holding at 10.1% of AUM; no specific news or performance commentary provided.

title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of EPAM Systems, Inc. - EPAM
author: Pomerantz Llp
description: Pomerantz Law Firm is investigating potential securities fraud claims against EPAM Systems following the company's disclosure on February 19, 2026 that its largest NEORIS customer is ramping down business. The announcement triggered a 6.61% stock price decline ($9.20 per share) over two trading sessions. The firm is also investigating similar claims against Chemours Company and Alight, Inc.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company disclosed significant revenue decline from largest NEORIS customer with expected further ramp down, leading to 6.61% stock price drop and triggering securities fraud investigation by major law firm
Subject of securities fraud investigation by Pomerantz Law Firm, though specific details are not provided in the article
Subject of securities fraud investigation by Pomerantz Law Firm, though specific details are not provided in the article
